prerequisites: A Salesloft account with access to Your Applications settings (API key creation is available to Salesloft customers; partners must use OAuth).
A start date (start_date), the earliest date from which records are synced; required.
Credentials (credentials) for one of the supported authentication methods; required.
For API key authentication: an API key created under Your Applications with at least one scope selected.
For OAuth: a registered OAuth application providing a Client Id and Client Secret, plus a completed Authorization Code grant that yields an access token and refresh token.

authMethods.name: API Key (credentials.api_key)
authMethods.summary: Create an API key in Salesloft under Your Applications > API Keys > Create New, giving it a descriptive name and at least one scope. Keys are tied to the issuing user and have the form ak_ followed by a 64-character hex string. The key is sent on each request as Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY. This method is intended for Salesloft customers; partners must use OAuth.

authMethods.name: OAuth Authorization Code (credentials.client_id / client_secret + access_token / refresh_token)
authMethods.summary: Register an OAuth application under Your Applications > OAuth Applications > Create New (name, description, redirect URI, type, Authorization Code grant, and scopes) to obtain a Client Id and Client Secret. Send users to https://accounts.salesloft.com/oauth/authorize to authorize, then exchange the returned code at https://accounts.salesloft.com/oauth/token for an access_token (expires after 7200 seconds) and a refresh_token used to renew it. Requests use Authorization: Bearer YOUR_ACCESS_TOKEN.

setupSteps.title: Choose an authentication method
setupSteps.body: Pick API key authentication if you are a Salesloft customer, or OAuth (Authorization Code) if you are a partner or need delegated, refreshable access. Both methods authenticate API requests with an Authorization: Bearer header.

setupSteps.title: Create an API key (API key method)
setupSteps.body: In Salesloft, go to Your Applications > API Keys > Create New. Provide a descriptive name and select at least one required scope, then save to receive the key. Keys look like ak_ followed by a 64-character hex string and are tied to your user, so keep them secret and never embed them in front-end code. Provide this value as credentials.api_key.

setupSteps.title: Register an OAuth application (OAuth method)
setupSteps.body: In Salesloft, go to Your Applications > OAuth Applications > Create New. Provide a name, description, and redirect URI, choose the application type, select the Authorization Code grant, and choose the required scopes. On submission you receive an Application Id (Client Id) and Secret (Client Secret); supply the secret as credentials.client_secret.

setupSteps.title: Complete the OAuth Authorization Code flow (OAuth method)
setupSteps.body: Direct the user to https://accounts.salesloft.com/oauth/authorize?client_id=YOUR_CLIENT_ID&redirect_uri=YOUR_REDIRECT_URI&response_type=code to approve access. Exchange the returned authorization code by POSTing your client credentials and the code to https://accounts.salesloft.com/oauth/token. This returns an access_token (expires_in 7200 seconds) and a refresh_token. Supply these as credentials.access_token and credentials.refresh_token; the access token is renewed using the refresh token without re-authorization.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector fields
setupSteps.body: Set the credentials field with the chosen authentication method's secrets, and set start_date to the earliest date from which Salesloft records should be synced. Both credentials and start_date are required.
